exemplar.title: M25 #4: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #4: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained, focused engagement with the prescribed title
Clear, coherent arguments supported by wellâchosen, discipline-specific examples
Effective linkage of examples back to the central knowledge question
Demonstrated evaluation of alternative perspectives and consideration of implications
weaknesses: Evaluation of historical examples is less thorough than that of the natural sciences
Some transitions between sections are abrupt
Occasional overstatements (e.g., about historyâs incapacity for tool-driven progress) indicating partial rather than full evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The strand assessment places the essay securely in band 7â8, reflecting very strong performance in understanding and developing the knowledge question through clear arguments and relevant examples. To conform to the immovable total of 6 marks, the single gradable criterion is awarded the full allocation of 6. This recognizes the essayâs high level of coherence, focus, and example use, while noting the minor unevenness in evaluation that prevents a theoretical maximum score.

annotations.comment: The conclusion consistently refers back to the prescribed title and maintains focus. This sustained relevance underscores your coherent engagement with the knowledge question.
You successfully connect the CRISPR example back to the prescribed title, showing coherence in your argument structure and clear engagement with the knowledge question.

The student provides a clear, operational definition of âimproved knowledgeâ as functional, usable, and explanatory. This anchors the discussion and aligns effectively with the prescribed title, demonstrating precise conceptual understanding.

The introduction remains quite broad and descriptive before defining key terms. Consider moving the thesis statement earlier to focus more directly on the knowledge question and reduce general exposition.
The transition from the descriptive opening to the formal definition is abrupt. Introduce connecting phrases to improve flow and maintain coherence between general context and your defined terms.
This sentence fragment is incomplete (âSolid technological reliance is the onlyâ). Ensure all sentences are fully written to avoid confusing the reader and to maintain clarity.

The CT perfusion example is well-chosen and precisely explained, demonstrating how a technological tool yields functional improvements in diagnostic knowledge. This concrete illustration strengthens the natural sciences discussion.
Consider integrating quantitative data on diagnostic accuracy and treatment outcomes to bolster your example and deepen the evaluation of knowledge reliability.
The transition phrase âOn the other handâ is functional but simplistic. Use a more explicit signal of contrast that ties the discussion back to the central question and maintains cohesion.
You could introduce counterarguments about overreliance on AI, considering how automated interpretation might lead to new forms of epistemic uncertainty.
There is limited evaluation of global accessibility constraints on CT perfusion. Address how socioeconomic factors may restrict its usability to qualify claims about widespread knowledge improvement.

The discussion of ethical constraints is raised but not analysed to the same depth as the scientific benefits. Elaborate on long-term epistemic consequences of restricting research to strengthen evaluation.
The first-person interview with a former prisoner poignantly illustrates the loss of primary sources. This concrete anecdote personalises the argument about source attrition in history.

The description of CRISPR-Cas9 clearly shows its impact on explanatory knowledge in genetics. You explain capabilities and innovations effectively, linking the toolâs functions to theoretical advancements.

The claim that historical events are entirely irreproducible overstates the case. Introduce nuance by acknowledging how digital reconstruction and modelling can offer partial insights into lost contexts.
Improve the transition between natural sciences and history by summarising how insights from one domain inform the other, maintaining coherence across sections.
Employing first-person narrative undermines the essayâs objective tone. Reframe this anecdote in the third person or cite published oral histories to maintain academic formality.
The AI-driven decipherment example is well chosen and nuanced, acknowledging both its potential to reconstruct lost evidence and the credibility concerns due to lack of verifiable data.
Strengthen this section by referencing secondary literature on historiographical methods for preserving and interpreting oral testimonies.
The setup for the AI-decipherment example lacks an explicit link back to the guiding question. Preface it with a brief statement on how it challenges the âirreproducibilityâ assertion.
The discussion omits consideration of training-data bias in machine-learning models. Address how algorithmic limitations might affect the reliability of deciphered texts.
Draw an explicit connection between this AI example and the overall thesis to reinforce coherence and remind the reader of its relevance to the title.
The opening of the conclusion simply restates prior points without deeper reflection. Integrate analysis of the implications you have discussed to avoid mere summary.
The concluding paragraph is repetitive and does not synthesise the pro-con evaluations developed. Refine it to highlight the most significant insights and their implications for knowledge production.
Expand the reflection on ethical constraints in your conclusion to deepen the evaluation and demonstrate clear awareness of how moral concerns shape knowledge outcomes.

The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
